name: Raspberry-Apricot Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Defrost the frozen raspberries and remove excess juice.
2. Place the drained apricots in the pie crust.
3. Spoon the drained raspberries over the apricots.
4. Pour the reserved raspberry juice over the top layer of fruit.
5. In a small bowl, mix together the sugar, flour, and salt.
6. Sprinkle this mixture evenly over the berries.
7. Dot the surface with pieces of butter.
8. Scatter the almond extract on top.
9. Place the second crust on top of the filling and trim any excess pastry.
10. Brush the edges with a little water and seal the edges.
11. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 50-60 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the filling is bubbling.
12. You may substitute 2 1/2 cups fresh blackberries and 5 fresh peaches for raspberries and apricots. Increase sugar to 1 cup if desired.
13. Serves 6-8.
